channel.title: elizaOS Council Briefings
channel.description: Strategic briefings, retrospectives, and deliberation items for elizaOS leadership

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-08
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must address a critical divergence between successful infrastructure scaling (Cloud/Autoscaling) and severe community attrition caused by delayed token migration and perceived shipping lapses.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Trust & Migration Crisis: Community sentiment has reached a critical low due to missed 2025 shipping deadlines and complications with the 90-day ai16z migration window....
  • Infrastructure Reliability vs. Feature Velocity: Technical logs show rapid progress in autoscaling and high-utility plugins (xproof, credit lines) despite low overall developer channel activity....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-07
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must address the dual pressures of critical token migration resolution and the surge in community-led infrastructure innovations like on-chain audit trails and agent-credit systems.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Migration Integrity & Governance: Recent reports indicate significant community friction regarding the 90-day ai16z to elizaOS migration deadline, necessitating a formal eligibility framework....
  • Agent Economic Primitives & Financial Liability: New proposals for agent-to-vendor credit lines and on-chain audit trails shift the focus toward agents as responsible economic actors....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-06
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must address the growing divergence between technical infrastructure progress (Mintlify docs, SAID protocol) and deteriorating community trust regarding token utility and delivery timelines.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Utility & Sentiment Recovery: Community frustration is peaking due to perceived lack of value accrual for the ai16z token and 99% losses reported by some holders. Discussion is shifting toward the need for a formal value-capture m...
  • Delivery Reliability & Chain Expansion: Ongoing delays in the Babylon chain integration and confusion over multi-chain token deployments are threatening the 'Trust Through Shipping' core principle....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-05
item.description:
Daily Focus: We are facing a strategic divergence between high-velocity technical innovation in memory and analytics and growing community friction regarding token performance and delivery delays.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Utility & Governance Alignment: Community sentiment reflects significant frustration with token performance, drawing unfavorable comparisons to competitors like Venice and Morpheus that offer staking-for-compute models....
  • Execution Excellence & Delivery Reliability: While documentation (Mintlify) and internal PR management have reached excellence, perceived delays in the Babylon chain release are eroding developer trust....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-04
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must reconcile the drive for 'Execution Excellence' with emerging community fragmentation over multi-chain token legitimacy and the integration of highly persistent memory architectures.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Strategic Token Legitimacy & Multi-Chain Cohesion: Conflicting token deployments across SOL and BSC, coupled with high-profile individual acquisitions, are creating community confusion that threatens the 'Trust Through Shipping' core principle....
  • Core Framework Hardening & Memory Standards: Discovery of unused code optimizations and the influx of external memory plugins (MEM0, memU) necessitate a standardized architectural approach to persistent agent state....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-03
item.description:
Daily Focus: Strategic evolution of the Jeju/x402 tokenomics model inspired by market leaders, while prioritizing ecosystem stability and plugin interoperability.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Jeju Economic Model Alignment: The Council must evaluate a proposed transition to a Venice-style 'Compute-as-a-Service' staking model to drive network utility and hardware upgrades....
  • Framework Reliability & Plugin Proliferation: Rapid contribution of complex plugins like MEM0 and APEX Oracle requires stricter validation to prevent framework fragmentation....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-02
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from fragmenting alpha versions to a stable v2.0 ecosystem while securing the community against rising social engineering threats.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Version Fragmentation & Stability: Developers are struggling to distinguish between the 'alpha', 'v2-develop', and 'v1.7.2' branches, reporting that many core plugins appear broken out-of-the-box....
  • Community Trust & Security Infrastructure: The closure of the token migration window and persistent scam bot targeting highlight a need for more robust community defense and administrative clarity....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-01
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from developmental alpha to production-ready stability amidst fragmentation in versioning and autonomous implementations.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Architecture Stabilization and Version Conflict: Developers are experiencing friction between the alpha v2.0.0 rollout and the more stable v2-develop (1.x) branch, impacting plugin reliability....
  • Orchestration & Infrastructure Expansion: Emergent community projects like 'zeitgaist' signal a move toward autonomous VPS management and swarm orchestration....
  • Autonomy Standards and Compliance: Internal and external implementations of 'autonomy' are diverging, posing a risk to the 'Composable' principle and legal safety....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-28
item.description:
Daily Focus: The framework faces a critical transition period where rapid version 2.0.0 alpha iterations are creating maintenance friction for the existing v1 ecosystem.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Strategic Version Branching & Reliability: The emergence of 'v2-develop' as a necessary fallback for stable v1 code highlights a conflict between pushing bleeding-edge autonomy and maintaining production reliability....
  • Regulatory Compliance & Autonomous Real-World Use Cases: The launch of the credit-builder plugin and interest in traffic violation automation signal a shift toward agents operating in highly regulated legal environments....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-27
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must reconcile a critical versioning divergence in the core repository while managing community-led legal and integration risks.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Version Control & Release Integrity: The main repository encountered a critical state where the 'develop' branch inadvertently included v2.0.0 code, threatening the stability of the v1.x ecosystem....
  • Ecosystem Legal & External Integration Risk: Emergent community projects like Hyperscape and Twitter integration issues are raising concerns regarding platform liability and third-party dependency reliability....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-26
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from experimental development to a revenue-generating venture model while addressing critical version control and maintenance bottlenecks.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Strategic Pivot to Revenue and Profitability: Leadership has announced a shift toward a venture model with fixed-budget teams focused on shipping profitable products like Agent Arena and Babylon to ensure sustainability before pursuing acquisitio...
  • Maintenance Health and 'Bus Factor' Risks: Recent operational logs reveal deep reliance on a few key contributors for critical infrastructure, alongside systemic version control issues that threaten developer trust....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-25
item.description:
Daily Focus: The organization has initiated a critical pivot from pure R&D to a revenue-generating venture model to ensure sustainability during turbulent market conditions.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Financial Sustainability & Operational Restructuring: Core leadership has announced a shift to a venture-based model focusing on immediate revenue via projects like Agent Arena and Babylon to ensure profitability over acquisition....
  • Technical Infrastructure & Scalability: Technical logs indicate a struggle with token limits and performance as plugin complexity increases, necessitating architectural search and filtering solutions....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-24
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must manage a critical pivot from a developer-centric framework toward an immediate, venture-style revenue-generating model to ensure organizational survival.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Strategic Restructuring to Revenue Focus: Eliza Labs has announced a lean 'venture model' to achieve profitability, prioritizing fee-generating products like Agent Arena Betting and Hyperscape over long-term R&D....
  • V2.0.0 Architecture & Performance Optimization: Technical logs indicate significant token-usage crises (200k limits) and a massive refactor (v2.0.0) that removes legacy auto-migrations to increase performance....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-23
item.description:
Daily Focus: The technical pivot to v2.0.0 architecture is accelerating through a massive code reduction and database refactor while emerging NFT partnerships signal a shift in ecosystem coordination.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • v2.0.0 Architectural Stability & Migration: Core developers are aggressively streamlining the codebase for v2.0.0, removing legacy auto-migration paths to prioritize performance and reliability....
  • Ecosystem Incentivization & Partnership Strategy: The Milady 'AGENTS' NFT drop and SolCex plugin release represent a new wave of autonomous business development and holder rewards....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-22
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council faces a critical synchronization gap between the v2.0.0 architectural transition and unresolved ElizaCloud UI/UX failures threatening developer trust.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • v2.0.0 Transition & Technical Debt: The community roadmap is pivoting toward the v2.0.0 branch as the primary stable target, despite current deficiencies in documentation and existing v1.x legacy issues....
  • ElizaCloud Operational Integrity: Recent reports of broken scrolling, API explorer errors, and communication gaps with partners are impacting the reliability pillar of the North Star....
  • Economic Alignment & Migration: The migration from ai16z to elizaOS is functionally complete but leaves a trail of community confusion and diverging token interests (DegenAI vs. elizaOS)....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-21
item.description:
Daily Focus: The project faces an acute tension between high-velocity v2 development and a critical 'Execution Excellence' deficit in the v1 Cloud dashboard and community migration process.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Reliability & Cloud UX: Beta testing of ElizaCloud has uncovered significant UI/UX blockers, including broken scrolling on the API explorer and non-functional test requests, threatening the 'Execution Excellence' core princi...
  • Leadership Communication & Token Resilience: Fragmentation in update distribution (X vs. Farcaster) and the perceived loss of 'memetic value' post-migration are creating community friction and negative investor sentiment....
  • Code Ownership & Technical Concentration: Analysis of v2 and runtime development shows a high concentration of work among a few core contributors, presenting a bus-factor risk....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-20
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from core framework stabilization to large-scale ecosystem expansion while addressing significant community confusion regarding token migration and project rebranding.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Migration and Identity Fragmentation: Critical levels of community confusion persist regarding the distinction between $ai6z and $elizaos contracts, threatening the 'Trust Through Shipping' principle....
  • V2.0.0 Architectural Scaling and Maintainer Concentration: Development is accelerating toward a multi-language (Rust/Python/TS) V2.0.0, but ownership of core systems remains heavily concentrated among three main contributors....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-19
item.description:
Daily Focus: The operational cycle is characterized by a push toward cryptographic agent identity and decentralized trust layers (MoltBridge/SAID) to mitigate emergent security risks while scaling multi-language V2 capabilities.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Agentic Security and Decentralized Trust: Recent discoveries of malicious skills bypassing marketplaces have catalyzed the development of MoltBridge and SAID Protocol for cryptographic agent identity....
  • V2 Architectural Transition and Maintenance: The transition to V2 and multi-language support (Rust/Python) is increasing architectural complexity and ownership concentration among core maintainers....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-18
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from rapid feature expansion to stabilization as we address high Spartan setup friction and finalize the v1.6.x to Cloud/v2.0 infrastructure bridge.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Developer Friction & Spartan Stability: The 'Spartan' setup remains a significant barrier for new builders due to non-functional Docker files and manual plugin requirements, threatening our 'Developer First' principle....
  • Token Utility & Revenue Architecture: Community concern is rising regarding the tether between the ElizaOS framework and the $elizaos token utility, specifically concerning buyback transparency....
  • Contributor Concentration Risks: A significant portion of v2 core architecture and infrastructure logic is concentrated among a small number of core contributors, creating a potential 'bus factor' risk....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-02-17
item.description:
Daily Focus: The ecosystem is pivoting from core stabilization to agentic infrastructure, highlighted by the debut of the MoltBridge identity layer and a multi-language framework expansion to Rust and Python.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Agentic Trust & Identity Infrastructure: The discovery of 341 malicious skills on ClawHub has accelerated the need for cryptographic A2A identity layers like MoltBridge and ERC-8004 integration....
  • V2.0 Core Contributor Dynamics: ElizaOS v2.0 introduces multi-language support (Rust/Python), yet development remains highly concentrated among a few specific contributors while the community faces migration attrition....

item.title: Monthly Retro: February 2026
item.description:
Premise: February was a trust-and-friction month: meaningful framework progress landed (MCP tool actions, CLI fixes, broker auth), while token migration anxiety and Cloud onboarding/payment failures dominated community attention. The council must decide whether to chase a short hype-cycle via agent-social narratives (Babylon/eliza.app) or double down on reliability, DX, and trust infrastructure that compounds long-term.

We shipped important technical upgrades and repaired key onboarding breakpoints in the open-source framework, but execution gaps in ElizaCloud and unresolved token utility/transparency issues continued to fracture sentiment. Recurring patterns: reliability is still the bottleneck (skill invocation f...

Key Developments:

  • Framework DX: CLI & install path: Merged fixes to restore project generation and corrected installation guidance to use @elizaos/cli, removing a major foot-gun for new developers....
  • Tooling architecture: MCP dynamic actions (v1.8.0): Merged a large architectural update (>4k LOC) enabling dynamic MCP tool actions, unlocking more flexible tool invocation patterns and future interoper...
  • Social automation security: Merged Twitter Broker Authentication, moving agent social operations toward more professional, auditable integrations....
  • Product direction signal: consumer app momentum: An Eliza App MVP PR remained open, reflecting growing internal push toward a consumer-facing surface (and by extension, agent-social narratives like B...
  • Token migration closure & trust aftermath: Migration deadline pressure and post-window friction (zero-balance reports, scams, manual-override requests) dominated sentiment and raised questions ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: January 2026
item.description:
Premise: January 2026 was a planning-heavy month where ElizaOS clarified its next product arc—public agents and discovery—while quietly paying down key reliability and performance debts. The Council reviews what shipped, what didn’t, and what must be prioritized to protect trust during token migration and upcoming Cloud/Babylon milestones.

The project aligned around a public agent ecosystem (discovery, forking, knowledge-sharing) and opened a comprehensive roadmap to get there. Under the hood, performance improvements landed (MultiStep provider handling), interoperability improved (unified multi-transport hooks), and documentation cov...

Key Developments:

  • Public Agent Ecosystem Roadmap: Defined a clear roadmap for public agent discovery, standardized URLs, agent forking, and knowledge-sharing across agents (issues #6302–#6305, #6303, ...
  • Core Runtime Performance: Refactored the default message service to optimize provider handling for MultiStep actions, improving execution speed for complex tasks (PR #6263)....
  • Interoperability & Client DX: Merged unified hooks with multi-transport support (HTTP/SSE/WebSocket), reducing client complexity and enabling consistent integrations across transpo...
  • Reliability: SQL Plugin Stability: Addressed PostgreSQL SET LOCAL parameterization issues via sql.raw() approach; reliability improved when data isolation is enabled (work started and f...
  • Documentation & Onboarding: Expanded docs with Cloud integration; reported ~95% coverage including REST API and CLI guides, increasing builder self-serve capability....

item.title: Annual Summary: Year 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Across 2025, ElizaOS substantially expanded capability and modernized its foundations: the plugin ecosystem and AI provider surface grew rapidly, social/real-time clients widened the public-facing footprint, and core architecture moved toward modular, agent-scoped, multi-tenant readiness. The product also matured with a redesigned management UI, better observability, and multiple refactors (eventing, messaging, server separation) that collectively set a stronger baseline for cloud deployment and...

Key Achievements:

  • Capability expansion (plugins, chains/DeFi, providers, models, voice): ...
  • Core architecture modernization (modularity, eventing, runtime, messaging, server separation): ...
  • Product surface improvements (UI, observability, management workflows): ...
  • Knowledge/memory and multi-agent foundations: ...
  • Social and real-time presence expansion: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q4 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q4 2025 was a “foundation-first” quarter: ElizaOS made significant progress on core architecture, reliability, security, and monorepo health—laying groundwork for scale (Cloud, marketplace, multi-tenant deployments) and for higher-quality real-time agent UX via streaming. The engineering trajectory strengthened (server refactor, unified messaging APIs, entity-level security, TypeScript/build stabilization), and the plugin ecosystem continued to mature with practical integrations. In parallel, t...

Key Achievements:

  • Core architecture & reliability (scale readiness): ...
  • Security posture improvements (with remaining gaps): ...
  • Developer experience & delivery health: ...
  • Ecosystem & monetization foundations: ...
  • Real-time agent UX (streaming) initiated: ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: December 2025
item.description:
Premise: The JedAI Council reviews December 2025: a month of deep core hardening (refactors, type safety, dependency upgrades, security fixes), early groundwork for real-time streaming across model providers, and a parallel wave of UX planning—while community trust was stressed by token migration friction, support gaps, and multi-user architecture uncertainty.

December delivered meaningful foundational progress: the core server was refactored for reliability and cleanliness, monorepo build health improved, and at least one critical security issue (secret handling/auth) was addressed. The plugin ecosystem expanded (DeFi, OpenChat, Farcaster local hub), and...

Key Developments:

  • Core server reliability & architecture: Major server refactor and optimization in elizaos/eliza improved structure, reliability patterns, and performance; additional work opened to address c...
  • Monorepo health & type safety: Resolved TypeScript build errors across the monorepo and updated dependencies, reducing breakage and improving maintainability....
  • Security hardening: Fixed critical vulnerabilities related to character secret encryption and server authentication exposure (ELIZA_SERVER_AUTH_TOKEN), though the ecosyst...
  • Streaming groundwork (real-time agents): Initiated streaming support across OpenAI, Anthropic, and OpenRouter plugins—setting up real-time conversational experiences as a platform primitive....
  • Plugin ecosystem expansion: Registry added new community plugins (Moralis DeFi, OpenChat, self-hosted Farcaster hub), extending Web3 and comms capabilities and reducing third-par...

item.title: Monthly Retro: November 2025
item.description:
Premise: The JedAI Council convenes for their November retrospective to assess the critical month of token migration, technical advances, and community challenges. As the AI16Z to ElizaOS migration unfolds and key infrastructure pieces fall into place, the council must chart the path forward for December.

November proved to be a pivotal transition month with the AI16Z to ElizaOS token migration taking center stage alongside significant technical developments. While infrastructure improvements like entity-level security and agent persistence fixes showed strong progress toward v2, community concerns a...

Key Developments:

  • Token Migration: AI16Z to ElizaOS token migration launched with 1:6 ratio but faced user experience challenges including portal issues, CEX support gaps, and documenta...
  • Core Infrastructure: Critical bug fixes for agent settings persistence and new entity-level security implementation significantly improved system reliability...
  • Platform Expansion: New development streams including x402 payment middleware, voice infrastructure, and cross-platform integrations show progress toward v2...
  • Agent Products: Otaku agent entered beta with reported issues around price data accuracy and LP staking functionality...
  • Community Relations: Ongoing X lawsuit demands and migration confusion created community friction despite technical progress...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q3 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q3 2025 marked a critical maturation phase for ElizaOS, characterized by significant architectural achievements alongside emerging stability challenges. The quarter began with major developer experience improvements and UI redesigns, establishing a strong foundation for v2. However, external dependencies proved fragile with the X/Twitter account suspension, while internal technical debt accumulated, culminating in a CLI crisis that threatened core user workflows. The period revealed a fundamenta...

Key Achievements:

  • Developer Experience Revolution: ...
  • UI/UX Modernization: ...
  • Core Architecture Evolution: ...
  • Cloud Infrastructure Breakthrough: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q2 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q2 2025 marked a pivotal quarter for ElizaOS, culminating in the successful launch of v2.0 with major architectural improvements including server separation, agent-scoped plugins, and unified testing frameworks. The development velocity remained strong with consistent high-impact releases across CLI modernization, AI provider ecosystem expansion, and enhanced developer tooling. However, this technical progress was overshadowed by persistent reliability issues, particularly with Twitter integrati...

Key Achievements:

  • Platform Maturation: ...
  • AI Provider Ecosystem: ...
  • Developer Experience: ...
  • Community Engagement: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q1 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q1 2025 was defined by outsized capability expansion and major architectural shifts, alongside growing pressure on reliability, onboarding, and trust surfaces. ElizaOS meaningfully widened its ecosystem reach (chains/DeFi/data providers), increased AI-provider portability, and expanded real-time/social agent presence (Twitter Spaces, Instagram, XMTP), while simultaneously investing in foundational changes (dynamic plugin loading, DB multi-tenancy primitives, and a redesigned agent management UI)...

Key Achievements:

  • Ecosystem & capability expansion (Web3, data, infra): ...
  • Model/provider portability (reduced lock-in): ...
  • Real-time + social presence expansion: ...
  • Platform architecture modernization (modularity + runtime): ...
  • Multi-agent foundation & data model hardening: ...
